components.name: Widget Container
components.description: Standard container for the desktop widget
components.html:
Quota
350 / 500
Updated 3m ago

narrative.overview: A minimal, high-contrast desktop widget that blends into the background of a developer's desktop. It mimics the clean, utilitarian, and distraction-free dark gray aesthetic of GitHub and the Antigravity IDE.
keyCharacteristics: Sleek Utility: Flat dark grays with a sharp gold-orange progress bar indicator.
Glanceability: Clear, highly legible text sizes that can be scanned in milliseconds.
Atmospheric State: Changes contrast and saturation dynamically when the network or API state changes.

rules.name: The Singularity Rule
rules.body: Only the active progress bar and current remaining count may use the Active Gold accent. All other labels and metadata must remain in Neutral colors.

rules.name: The Clean Line Rule
rules.body: All text elements must use uppercase for small labels under 10px to ensure maximum crispness and block-alignment at small sizes.

rules.name: The Flat-Surface Rule
rules.body: No drop shadows are allowed on the widget container. Separation from the wallpaper is achieved strictly via the thin Border Gray outline and the solid GitHub Dark background.

dos: Do desaturate the widget (opacity 0.6, colors shifted to grayscale) when the application cache is outdated or offline.
Do keep the widget size fixed to 150x80px to fit standard desktop grid spacings.
Do align the progress bar flush with the bottom or side of the layout.
donts: Don't use any card drop shadows or container blurs (glassmorphism).
Don't use neon green or neon blue gradient highlights.
Don't allow mouse clicks or hover triggers on the widget itself while on the desktop (keep click-through enabled).
